channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Iberdrola y AWS estrechan lazos: de la compra de energía verde a la nube
item.description: Iberdrola ha ampliado el acuerdo de colaboración que mantenía con el gigante tecnológico Amazon Web Services (AWS) haciendo hincapié en el mercado de Iberia.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The number of snakes removed from this Australian yard will make you shudder
item.description: A man described feeling “the shudders” as more than 100 venomous red-bellied black snakes were removed from a pile of mulch in his Sydney backyard.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Like TikTok, lawmakers want to ban DeepSeek from government devices
item.description: A bipartisan duo in the the U.S. House is proposing legislation to ban the Chinese artificial intelligence app DeepSeek from federal devices, similar to the policy already in place for the popular social media platform TikTok. Reps. Josh Gottheimer, D-N.J., and Darin LaHood, R-Ill.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: New law in Brazil is making students put away their smartphones at school
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Brazilian students returned to class this week with a new task: staying away from their smartphones as a new law restricting their use in schools took effect.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: OpenAI looks across US for sites to build its Trump-backed Stargate AI data centers
item.description: OpenAI is scouring the U.S. for sites to build a network of huge data centers to power its artificial intelligence technology, expanding beyond a flagship Texas location and looking across 16 states to accelerate the Stargate project championed by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Matt O'Brien, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Judge blocks Trump plan for federal worker resignations
item.description: Billionaire Elon Musk, one of President Donald Trump's most powerful advisers, has orchestrated an unprecedented financial incentive for people to leave their government jobs, promising several months of pay in return for their resignation.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Veeam backup software has a serious security flaw - here's how to stay safe
item.description: Multiple backup products found carrying a critical severity flaw allowing malicious actors to run arbitrary commands on compromised endpoints.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Oracle’s HR software now has AI agents to help with taxes and career planning
item.description: Oracle’s new AI agents will answer employee questions about everything job-related, from hiring to retiring.
item.dc:creator: Jocelyn Mintz, Fast Company

item.title: One Tech Tip: How to block your phone from tracking your location
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Smartphones are useful tools for everyday life, but they're privy to nearly everything about you, including all the places you've been — if you let them.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Google scraps its diversity hiring goals as it complies with Trump's new government contractor rules
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google is scrapping some of its diversity hiring targets, joining a lengthening list of U.S. companies that have abandoned or scaled back their diversity, equity and inclusion programs.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: DOGE’s ‘unimpeded’ access to classified data poses national, economic security risks
item.description: There is no information on who has been hired under DOGE, the authority under which it operates, or how it vets and monitors its staff. The post DOGE’s ‘unimpeded’ access to classified data poses national, economic security risks first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Obis, Federal News Network

item.title: How to minimize your digital footprint when you travel
item.description: Kurt the CyberGuy offers tips to dramatically reduce the risk of having your information stolen while booking flights, cruises, vehicles and accommodations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Databricks acquires BladeBridge to aid data warehouse migrations
item.description: Data lakehouse provider Databricks has acquired data platform modernization software provider BladeBridge for an undisclosed sum to help its new customers move from rival data warehouses, such as Teradata, Snowflake, Amazon Redshift, and Microsoft SQL Server, to its lakehouse built atop Databricks S
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Smart Traffic Enforcement: Kazakhstan’s Qorgau System in Action
item.description: Traffic management is a growing challenge for cities worldwide, requiring a balance between enforcement, efficiency, and public trust. In Kazakhstan, the Qorgau system is redefining road safety through an innovative fusion of artificial intelligence (AI), computer vision, and mobile technology.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Google's rising holiday's season ad sales aren't enough to ease worries about AI letdown
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google's digital ad sales continued to grow at a healthy clip during the holiday season, but that wasn't enough to offset investors' worries about whether its big bet on artificial intelligence will be lucrative as once envisioned.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Hawley makes push to allow parents to sue tech companies
item.description: Sen. Josh Hawley (R-Mo.) said Tuesday he will introduce legislation to empower parents to sue leading social media companies over alleged harms to children and teens.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Cisco: Latest news and insights
item.description: Cisco is the dominant vendor in enterprise networking, and under CEO Chuck Robbins, it continues to shake things up. Its most recent mega-acquisition is its $28 billion purchase of Splunk, a bold move that adds revenue opportunities in observability, data analytics, AIOps, and genAI.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Canada's most populous province to pause retaliatory measures as US puts tariffs on hold
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — The leader of Ontario, Canada’s most populous province, said Monday he will pause all retaliatory measures against the United States after news broke that the threat of tariffs has been put on hold for a month.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Taiwan chip tariff would raise industry costs, analysts say
item.description: President Donald Trump continues to use tariffs like a club to get his way, and is threatening to place tariffs on computer chips made in Taiwan, warning the additional charges could reach as high as 100%.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Bill Gates shares his thoughts on vaccine backlash, Intel's woes and Google's antitrust battle
item.description: Although Microsoft founder Bill Gates sat down for an interview with The Associated Press to discuss his new memoir, “Source Code: My Beginnings,” he also shared his views on a variety of other topics, including vaccine conspiracy views about him, his thoughts on the struggles of longtime computer c
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: SoftBank and OpenAI set up joint company to push artificial intelligence services
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japanese technology giant SoftBank Group and OpenAI stepped up their AI partnership Monday, setting up a 50-50 held company called SB OpenAI Japan.
item.dc:creator: YURI KAGEYAMA, Associated Press, NewsNation
